title: Emergent Abilities of Large Language Models
task: survey of emergent capabilities in LLMs

defines.name: emergent abilities of large language models
defines.definition: Abilities that are absent in smaller-scale models but present in larger-scale models, and therefore cannot be predicted by extrapolating smaller-model performance.

problem: Scaling laws explain many gains in language models, but some downstream abilities appear unpredictably only at larger scales and cannot be anticipated from smaller models.
